mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly increasing present equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines across all tested periods, with Rank IC consistently below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 6.0 percent, labor market deterioration signals imminent economic contraction, triggering risk-off sentiment that drives negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent week.
failure_condition: SPX forward 5-day returns remain positive in over 55 percent of instances where UNRATE crosses above 6.0 percent, or the signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, which translates into higher corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns when both series rise concurrently, with out-of-sample accuracy dropping below 50 percent or Rank IC falling below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion, industrial production growth outpaces capacity utilization, creating supply bottlenecks that fuel inflationary pressures and force central banks to tighten policy, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth during late-cycle phases correlates positively with 20-day equity returns, or the mechanism shows no predictive edge with Rank IC below 0.02 when capacity utilization exceeds 85 percent.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 210.0, persistent inflation erodes real corporate margins and forces higher discount rates, immediately suppressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 60 percent of observations where CPIAUCSL crosses above 210.0, or the threshold signal yields a negative Rank IC over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising nonfarm payrolls and falling unemployment rates confirms genuine labor market tightening, which supports wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined payroll-unemployment signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns when both conditions are met, with out-of-sample directional accuracy below 52 percent or Rank IC consistently below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth reflects underlying economic expansion that directly boosts corporate profit margins, creating a persistent positive drift in S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show no statistically significant correlation with 20-day forward equity returns across all tested periods, with Rank IC remaining below 0.01 over rolling 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Following a macroeconomic shock, a sustained rebound in industrial production signals supply chain normalization and capacity utilization recovery, which reduces input costs and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: INDPRO rebounds during post-shock periods fail to predict positive 5-day equity returns, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below 50 percent when global supply chain indices remain elevated.
